my-app Architecture Documentation

Overview:
my-app is a Python microservice deployed on Kubernetes cluster prod-01.
It provides REST API for user management and data processing.

Components:
- Backend: Python Flask application running in Docker containers
- Database: PostgreSQL 15 for persistent data storage
- Cache: Redis for session management and caching
- Deployment: ArgoCD manages deployments on Kubernetes
- Source: GitHub repository at company/my-app

Infrastructure:
- Kubernetes namespace: production
- Kubernetes cluster: prod-01
- vSphere VMs: Running Kubernetes nodes
- Load balancer: NGINX ingress controller

Troubleshooting Guide:

When my-app is not responding, check in this order:

1. Check ArgoCD sync status
   - Verify deployment status is Synced
   - Check health status is Healthy
   - Look for recent sync errors

2. Verify Kubernetes pod health
   - kubectl get pods -n production
   - kubectl describe pod my-app-xxx
   - Check pod events for issues

3. Check PostgreSQL connectivity
   - Verify database pod is running
   - Test connection from app pod
   - Check connection pool usage

4. Review recent GitHub commits
   - Look for changes in last deployment
   - Check if new code introduced issues

Common Issues:
- Database connection pool exhaustion (increase pool size)
- Redis cache full (increase memory limits)
- Pod memory limits exceeded (check OOMKilled events)
- Image pull errors (verify registry access)
- Network policies blocking traffic

Maintenance Procedures:
- Weekly: Review pod resource usage
- Monthly: Update base images
- Quarterly: Review and update documentation
